description:
A 911 police dispatcher needs a Talon voice command system that translates spoken incident codes into a standardized JSON log entry. Create a Talon module in C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\dispatch_system\ consisting of three files: dispatch_lists.py, dispatch_logger.py, and dispatch.talon.

In dispatch_lists.py, define a Module and Context with the following list mappings: user.call_priority ("code one" through "code four" mapping to "1" through "4"), user.incident_type ("burglary" -> "459", "robbery" -> "211", "homicide" -> "187", "assault" -> "240", "grand theft" -> "487"), and user.unit_designator ("adam" -> "A", "boy" -> "B", "david" -> "D", "lincoln" -> "L", "zebra" -> "Z").

In dispatch_logger.py, implement a user.log_dispatch(priority: str, incident: str, unit: str, zone: int) action. This action must generate a current ISO-8601 timestamp, format the unit and zone into a unit_id string with a zero-padded two-digit zone (e.g., unit "A" and zone 5 becomes "A-05"), and append a JSON dictionary containing the timestamp, priority, incident_code, and unit_id as a new line to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\dispatch_log.json.

In dispatch.talon, define the voice command "dispatch {user.call_priority} {user.incident_type} to unit {user.unit_designator} zone <number>" which calls the user.log_dispatch action using the captured variables in the correct order.
